channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nakuru court awards employee KSh 1.6 million after ruling her dismissal was procedurally unfair
item.description: A Nakuru court has awarded a senior accounts clerk, Margaret Karanja, over KSh 1.6 million after finding that her dismissal from Kentalya PLC was unlawful and procedurally unfair. Karanja, who earned ...

item.title: Ethiopia Advances Local Drug Manufacturing Through Stronger Regulations
item.description: Ethiopia is making big moves to improve its pharmaceutical sector by tightening regulations and boosting local drug production. The government wants to reduce dependence on imported medicines and make...

item.title: Rave reacts positively to Negi’s comment on Kapamilya Deal or No Deal
item.description: The entertainment segment from Kapamilya Deal or No Deal featured a light and engaging interaction between personalities Rave and Negi, which drew attention from viewers due to its humorous and lively...

item.title: Exploring Adolescence and Artistic Sensibilities in Denton Welch’s ‘In Youth is Pleasure’
item.description: Denton Welch’s novel, 'In Youth is Pleasure', first published in 1945 and republished by Penguin Classics in 2021, is a delicate and whimsical exploration of adolescent curiosity. The story revolves a...

item.title: Federal delegation visits Oyo following abduction of school pupils and teachers in Oriire LGA

item.title: Debate Emerges Over Inflation Metrics as Trump Fed Nominee Kevin Warsh Faces Criticism
item.description: A former insider has accused Donald Trump’s Federal Reserve chair nominee, Kevin Warsh, of using misleading methods to present inflation data in a more favorable light. The controversy stems from Wars...

item.title: Tanzania Smallholder Dairy Farmers Access Better Markets and Support Over a Million Families
item.description: Tanzania’s small-scale dairy farmers are now seeing better access to formal markets and modern facilities thanks to big improvements in the sector. For many years, farmers struggled with unreliable bu...

item.title: Angeles City hotel collapse death toll rises to 11 as retrieval operations continue
item.description: The death toll in the hotel collapse incident in Angeles City, Pampanga has risen to 11, according to the latest report. The tragic incident has prompted ongoing retrieval and recovery operations as a...

item.title: How football tracksuits help migrants preserve identity and community
item.description: Neal Corcoran, who migrated from Ireland to Australia in 1991, has built a unique personal and community story around football tracksuits, which he sees as far more than sportswear. Having grown up in...

item.title: Tiffany Trump visits historic Sonar Fort in Jaisalmer during Rajasthan trip
item.description: Tiffany Trump, daughter of US President Donald Trump, visited the historic Sonar Fort in Jaisalmer, Rajasthan, as part of her trip to India with her husband Michael Boulos. The couple arrived at Jaisa...

item.title: Soccer Aid 2026: England vs World XI line-ups, kick-off time and TV coverage
item.description: Soccer Aid returns for its 2026 edition with England taking on a World XI in a charity football match held at the London Stadium. The fixture, which has become a staple of the sporting and entertainme...

item.title: House spokesperson says due process is 'birthday gift' for VP Sara Duterte amid impeachment proceedings
item.description: A House of Representatives spokesperson has said that the “birthday gift” the chamber can offer Vice President Sara Duterte is the guarantee of due process as she faces an ongoing impeachment process....

item.title: Iran-US tensions deepen as nuclear talks face new setbacks under tougher U.S. proposal
item.description: Iran’s chief nuclear negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has stated that Tehran does not trust the United States and will not agree to any deal unless Iranian rights are fully protected. His comments ...

item.title: Israel Conducts Major Ground Operations in Lebanon Amid Escalating Hezbollah Attacks
item.description: Israel has significantly expanded its ground operations in Lebanon, marking its largest incursion into the country in 25 years. The Israeli Defence Forces (IDF) have crossed the Litani River and are n...

item.title: Proposed law aims to waive licensure and bar exam fees for indigent Filipinos
item.description: Isang panukalang batas na inihain ni Senator Erwin Tulfo ang naglalayong gawing libre ang board exams, bar exams, at civil service examinations para sa mga kwalipikadong indigent na Pilipino. Kilala b...

item.title: Falling birthrates, ageing populations and the political debate over immigration as a demographic solution
item.description: The article examines growing political and social concern over declining birthrates and ageing populations across the US, Europe and other developed regions. It highlights how prominent right-wing fig...

item.title: ICEED pushes clean cooking initiative as national priority in Nigeria

item.title: Atiku Meets Rival Hayatu-Deen in Lagos as ADC Moves to Heal Post-Primary Rifts
item.description: Former Vice President and African Democratic Congress (ADC) presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has held a closed-door meeting with his rival, Hayatu-Deen, at his residence in Lagos. The meeting i...

item.title: Renewed Beatlemania emerges in the UK ahead of Sam Mendes’ Beatles biopic series
item.description: The United Kingdom is experiencing a fresh surge of interest in The Beatles, with cultural commentators describing it as a new wave of Beatlemania ahead of a major series of biographical films directe...

item.title: Collingwood shed fire under investigation after no injuries reported
item.description: A small structure fire in Collingwood, Ontario, was brought under control without any reported injuries, according to local reporting. The incident involved a shed that caught fire in the early hours,...
